back_translation: When the people saw that Moses was delayed in coming down from the mountain, the people gathered around Aaron and said to him, 'Arise, make for us gods who will go before us; for as for this Moses, the man who brought us up from the land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him.'
Aaron said to them, 'Take off the gold earrings that are on the ears of your wives, your sons, and your daughters, and bring them to me.'
All the people took off the gold earrings that were on their ears and brought them to Aaron.
He took it from their hand and fashioned it with an engraving tool, and he made it into a molten calf. They said, 'These, O Israel, are your gods who brought you up from the land of Egypt!'
Aaron saw and built an altar before it, and Aaron called out and said, 'Tomorrow shall be a feast to the LORD.'
They rose early the next day and offered burnt offerings and brought peace offerings. The people sat down to eat and drink, and they rose up to play.
The LORD spoke to Moses, 'Go down, for your people whom you brought up from the land of Egypt have corrupted themselves.'

'They have turned aside quickly from the way that I commanded them; they have made for themselves a molten calf, and have bowed down to it, and sacrificed to it, and said, These, O Israel, are your gods who brought you up from the land of Egypt.'
The LORD said to Moses, 'I have seen this people, and behold, they are a stiff-necked people.'
'Now leave me alone, that my anger may burn against them and I may consume them; and I will make you into a great nation.'
Moses sought the favor of the LORD his God and said, 'O LORD, why does your anger burn against your people whom you brought out from the land of Egypt with great power and a mighty hand?'

'Why should the Egyptians say, saying, In evil he brought them out to kill them in the mountains and to consume them from the face of the earth? Turn from your fierce anger and relent from the evil to your people.'

'Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Israel your servants, to whom you swore by yourself and spoke to them: I will multiply your descendants like the stars of the heavens, and all this land that I have spoken of, I will give to your descendants, and they shall inherit it forever.'
The LORD relented from the evil that he had spoken of doing to his people.
Moses turned and came down from the mountain with the two tablets of the Testimony in his hand — tablets inscribed on both sides; on this side and on that side they were inscribed.
The tablets were the work of God, and the writing was the writing of God, engraved on the tablets.
Joshua heard the sound of the people as they shouted, and he said to Moses, 'The sound of war is in the camp.'
He said, 'It is not the sound of the shouting of victory, nor the sound of the shouting of defeat; the sound of singing I hear.'
When he came near to the camp and saw the calf and the dancing, Moses' anger burned. He threw the tablets from his hands and shattered them at the foot of the mountain.
He took the calf that they had made and burned it with fire, ground it until it was fine, and scattered it on the face of the water, and made the sons of Israel drink it.
Moses said to Aaron, 'What did this people do to you, that you have brought such great sin upon them?'
Aaron said, 'Let not the anger of my lord burn. You know the people — that they are set on evil.'
'They said to me, Make for us gods who will go before us, for as for this Moses, the man who brought us up from the land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him.'
'I said to them, Whoever has gold, take it off! And they gave it to me; and I threw it into the fire, and out came this calf.'
Moses saw the people, that they were running wild — for Aaron had let them run wild for a derision among their adversaries.
Moses stood at the gate of the camp and said, 'Whoever is for the LORD, to me!' All the sons of Levi gathered to him.

He said to them, 'Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: Each of you put his sword on his thigh; pass and return from gate to gate in the camp, and slay each his brother, each his friend, and each his neighbor.'
The sons of Levi did according to the word of Moses. About three thousand men of the people fell that day.
Moses said, 'Fill your hands today for the LORD, for each man was against his son and his brother, so that he may bestow a blessing upon you today.'
The next day Moses said to the people, 'You have sinned a great sin. Now I will go up to the LORD; perhaps I shall make atonement for your sin.'
Moses returned to the LORD and said, 'Oh, this people has sinned a great sin; they have made for themselves gods of gold.'
'But now, if you will forgive their sin — and if not, please blot me out from your book that you have written.'
The LORD said to Moses, 'Whoever has sinned against me, I will blot him out of my book.'
'Now go, lead the people to where I have spoken to you; behold, my angel shall go before you. But in the day of my reckoning, I will reckon their sin upon them.'
The LORD struck the people with a plague because of what they had done with the calf that Aaron had made.
